title: Sprint 5 Demo Rehearsal Pack
policy_note: Live demo transcript defaults are online-first. Offline questions in this pack are support-path or harness references for Admin rehearsal only.

questions.title: Leadership styles comparison
questions.prompt: What leadership styles are discussed and how do they differ?

expected_evidence.why: Primary narrative source for the leadership models used in the demo corpus.

expected_evidence.target: leadership comparison table covering transformational, transactional, and servant leadership
expected_evidence.why: The strongest answer should cite the contrast table instead of paraphrasing from memory.

questions.operator_note: Default transcript question. Use early in the live run to show multi-source synthesis with clear citations.

questions.title: Operating frequency range
questions.prompt: What is the operating frequency range?

expected_evidence.why: Expected primary source for the exact band numbers.

expected_evidence.target: operating band table or RF range section
expected_evidence.why: Answer quality is highest when the citation lands on the numeric table rather than a prose summary.

questions.operator_note: Good first-pass engineering question when you want an answer with exact numbers and a tight citation.

questions.title: Calibration interval and review cadence

expected_evidence.why: Expected maintenance source for the interval wording.

expected_evidence.target: section 7.3 calibration interval plus quarterly review cadence
expected_evidence.why: GUI smoke and offline/Admin rehearsal should land on the interval language, not only the general maintenance prose.

questions.operator_note: Offline/Admin support-path question. gui_demo_smoke uses this because it can be reproduced safely with a tiny synthetic corpus.

questions.title: Offline data egress posture
questions.prompt: Does any data leave the machine in offline mode?

expected_evidence.why: Expected summary source for the network boundary claims.

expected_evidence.target: localhost-only network boundary or zero-egress guarantee section
expected_evidence.why: Security reviewers will look for the exact wording behind the offline claim.

questions.operator_note: Use when the audience leans security-heavy and you need the answer to stay factual and source-bounded.

questions.title: Preventive maintenance impact
questions.prompt: What maintenance actions reduce downtime and how often are they reviewed?

expected_evidence.why: Expected cross-document source for the downtime reduction claim.

expected_evidence.target: quarterly review cycle tied to preventive maintenance program
expected_evidence.why: Strong answers should connect the action list with the review cadence rather than answer only one half.

questions.operator_note: Cross-document stress question. Best used after the quick wins when you want to show synthesis over multiple maintenance sources.
